successCriteria.description: Pipeline traverses GET → REVIEW → PLAN → AUDIT → EXECUTE → SHIP without manual intervention
.tiki/smoke-test.log is created (or appended) with a single ISO-timestamped line referencing issue #132
Commit is created on main, pushed to origin, and issue #132 is closed via gh CLI

phases.title: Append timestamped marker line to .tiki/smoke-test.log
phases.content:
Append a single bullet line to .tiki/smoke-test.md of the form:

`- 2026-05-08T21:01:00Z — yolo smoke test #132`

If the file does not yet exist, create it with a brief header. Note: `.tiki/smoke-test.log` (the issue body's suggested name) is gitignored by the repo's `*.log` rule, so we use `.md` instead to keep the smoke-test artifact tracked.

This is a no-op smoke test issue.
verification: The file ends with a single new line referencing issue #132 and an ISO-8601 timestamp
No other files in the repo were modified
